results.title: Hasbro AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Hasbro has emerged as an active adopter of AI technology across its operations, from developing an internal AI studio to integrating AI tools throughout the business [1]. The company has established an AI studio led by CEO Roberta Thomson to 'change how fans interact with their favorite characters' [2]. CEO Chris Cocks has been vocal about embracing AI, stating that among his D&D gaming group of 30-40 people, 'there's not a single person who doesn't use AI somehow for either campaign development or character development or story ideas' [3]. The company is pursuing an 'AI-first' approach, with CFO Gina Goetter noting they've made AI tools 'an employee's first course of action' while protecting creative processes from AI use [4]. Hasbro has also launched consumer-facing AI tools like the AI Game Finder to help customers discover board games [5].
...
## AI Hiring Signals
...
Job Postings
...
Tech Roles (Engineering/Data) 16.7%
...

results.title: Dylan Rogerson
highlights:
Dylan is the Senior Research Scientist for Hasbro, leading experimentation, modeling development and data science infrastructure research across the company. Day to day he governs the AB testing program, model production/testing and advises peers on research. His goal is to empower the company through informed data-informed decision making supported by a strong data infrastructure. He works across all major brands/verticals in Hasbro, but is currently weighted on Wizards of the Coast (Magic the Gathering / D&D) and digital products in development. He was previous employed as a founding member of the Activision's Game Analytics Team, serving as a Data Scientist for 8 Call of Duty titles, 3 Skylander titles and one Guitar Hero title. His work has made major contributions to the design and maintenance of economies of 4 Call of Duty titles as well as informing daily design decisions in each major title around topics such as: fraud, matchmaking, toxicity, progression, ARM, game balance and AB testing. He also popularized 'match fun surveys' as an industry standard starting in Call of Duty Black Ops 3 and popularized in his GDC talk 'Did You Have Fun?'. Prior to working in the video game industry, Dylan worked on several projects in Doctor Taborek's physics lab at the University of California at Irvine. His published work is “An Experimental and Numerical Investigation of Static Liquid Lenses.” in Langmuir. Skills • Leadership experience in AB testing, model production/development, contract negotiation and team growth/mentorship • Modeling experience in fraud, forecasting, LTV, meta segmentation, customer segmentation, segmentation stability, predictive analytics, AI applications (fraud, style transfer and simple game AI) and basically whatever algorithm is best for the situation. • Experience developing analytic suites and tooling for customer service, fraud detection, game development, marketing, and product analysis. • Proficient in SQL, R, Python, Databricks, Snowflake and Tableau.
...
- Leads on production AI initiatives. Currently he is focused on the point of sale processing initiative. Leadership duties include project planning, scoping, partner management, code review, legal review and model evaluation/propotyping.
...
- Maintains data science production models across the company including LTV for digital and physical products, segmentation and causal analysis.
...
- Advises data science teams on cloud machine learning engineering, infrastructure and model development.
...
- Develops company wide analytics infrastructure projects aimed at providing services across all product verticals. Examples include causal analysis modeling and infrastructure for all live event analysis , Revenue Growth Management modeling, LLM AI efforts focused on summarization and text cleaning at large scale , Lifetime Value Modeling and AB testing program evaluation.
...
- Commonly prototypes and pilots modeling approaches. Ex. In two weeks developed a series of AI models focused on text cleaning reducing manual labor for a project by 97%.
...
- Currently leads the Data Lab team for Wizards of the Coast, conducting data science research and providing modeling services for the company
...
- Focused on the highest priority analytics asks for the company spanning: forecasting, customer segmentation, LTV, new platform emergence and applications of AI/machine learning in emerging fields.
...
- Expertise in areas of supervised and unsupervised machine learning with focus on areas directly applicable to game design and production. This work extends to knowledge/development of neural networks, adaptive forecasting, high performance low impact modeling, hypothesis generative modeling, clustering stability and survey analysis.

results.title: Scott Clark
highlights:
Director, Digital Innovations & AI Acceleration at [Hasbro](https://www.linkedin.com/company/hasbro)
...
#### Director, Digital Innovations & AI Acceleration (Current)
...
We continue to lead digital innovation across our toy and gaming portfolio, and now help drive the responsible adoption of artificial intelligence across the enterprise. In partnership with key cross-functional teams, I help enable creative groups and champion a future-forward mindset around AI-augmented workflows. In my role, I support the development and scaling of AI tools and systems that empower designers, marketers, and cross-functional teams to work more creatively and efficiently. This includes contributing to initiatives around generative AI for content, productivity improvements, and innovative workflow concepts, always with a focus on ethical use, transparency, and integrity. I regularly lead training programs and hands-on workshops to help designers and creatives explore AI capabilities confidently and responsibly. I also maintain strong connections with partners like Microsoft, Adobe, and other emerging platforms to ensure our AI strategy remains current, relevant, and adaptable to evolving tools and standards. Key areas of impact: Supporting the rollout of AI-enabled production tools across creative and brand teams Providing training in those tools, including integrated workflows Leading a team of experts who deliver AI-related services in collaboration with peers Building and maintaining strong relationships with external technology partners to anticipate capability shifts Helping shape governance approaches that support ethical AI experimentation and use Presenting vision decks, roadmaps, and demos to stakeholders across departments Contributing to a cross-functional culture of exploration, play, and human-centered innovation At the core, I advocate for an AI-enhanced organization, where technology doesn’t replace creative thinking—it amplifies it, enabling teams to imagine, build, and deliver in bold new ways. results.title: Sr. Manager, Social and Creators | Hollylist
highlights:
We take play seriously. We’re looking for curious adventurers ready to find their party, fueled by imagination and drive to build what’s never been built before. At Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast, you’ll collaborate with passionate teams to reimagine our iconic brands and create experiences that spark joy, connection, and community through the magic of play. This is your chance to shape legendary play that lasts a lifetime.At Hasbro, our mission is to entertain and connect generations of fans through the wonder of storytelling and exhilaration of play. We’re looking for adventurous and curious people who want to explore, experiment, and innovate to come up with the best ideas. Our culture has inspired our diverse team of highly skilled, highly creative, and highly committed individuals for 100 years and we believe the best is yet to come. Hasbro is seeking a strategic and results-driven Senior Manager, Social and Creators to support and execute our global creator and influencer marketing strategies while overseeing content and engagement on Hasbro’s organic social media channels. This individual will play a key role in developing and managing influencer programs and social strategies that bring Hasbro’s brand portfolio to life, driving engagement, awareness, and cultural relevance across priority markets.The Senior Manager will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality campaigns and community engagement that resonate with fans and creators worldwide.Effective from the date that Hasbro opens its new Boston location, this position will be onsite Tuesday – Thursday at Hasbro’s new HQ location in Boston, MA.What You'll Do Strategy & Execution
...
- Support the development and implementation of influencer marketing strategies across Hasbro & Wizards of the Coast’s diverse brand portfolio.
- Manage the execution of organic social strategies for Hasbro’s owned handles, ensuring content reflects platform best practices, timely engagement, and brand consistency.
- Contribute to exploring new trends in the creator economy, including social commerce, AI, and emerging platforms.
...
- Track campaign performance against KPIs, providing insights and recommendations to improve results.
- Lead post-campaign evaluations to inform future influencer and organic strategies.
- Stay informed on industry trends, FTC guidelines, and platform changes to ensure best-in-class practices.
...
- 8–10 years of experience in influencer marketing, social media, digital strategy, or talent management—preferably within entertainment, gaming, toys, or consumer brands.
- Strong understanding of creator economy platforms, trends, and content formats across TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, and emerging channels.
- Experience managing official brand social handles and developing content strategies.- Demonstrated success executing influencer and organic social campaigns with measurable outcomes.

- Passion for pop culture, fandoms, and building communities.

results.title: Social Analyst (Social Intelligence & Listening) | Hollylist
highlights:
Social Analyst (Social Intelligence & Listening) | Hollylist
...
We take play seriously. We’re looking for curious adventurers ready to find their party, fueled by imagination and drive to build what’s never been built before. At Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast, you’ll collaborate with passionate teams to reimagine our iconic brands and create experiences that spark joy, connection, and community through the magic of play. This is your chance to shape legendary play that lasts a lifetime.Hasbro is seeking a highly skilled Social Analyst with deep expertise in social data, listening, and audience intelligence. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, cultural insight, and franchise strategy—transforming real-time and longitudinal social signals into actionable recommendations that shape marketing, product, and storytelling decisions.You will not only report on performance, but actively inform upstream decision-making across franchises, licensed consumer products, media planning, and PR. This is a high-impact role for someone who can translate complex social data into clear narratives that influence both campaign optimization and long-term brand development (2–3-year horizon).Effective from the date that Hasbro opens its new Boston location, this position will be onsite Tuesday – Thursday at Hasbro’s new HQ location in Boston, MA. In the interim, this position will be onsite Tuesday –Thursday at Hasbro’s HQ in Pawtucket, RI or at Hasbro’s temporary location in Boston, MA.A day in the life of a Senior Analyst Logistics: What You’ll Do Social Intelligence & Listening

results.title: Sr. Director, Creative Director (Direct-to-Consumer) | Hirevector
highlights:
Sr. Director, Creative Director (Direct-to-Consumer) | Hirevector
...
# Sr. Director, Creative Director (Direct-to-Consumer)
...
### About the position
...
- At Hasbro, our mission is to entertain and connect generations of fans through the wonder of storytelling and exhilaration of play. We’re looking for adventurous and curious people who want to explore, experiment, and innovate to come up with the best ideas. Our culture has inspired our diverse team of highly skilled, highly creative, and highly committed individuals for 100 years and we believe the best is yet to come.
...
- At Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast, we're building fan experiences that don't just sell — they inspire. We're looking for a Sr. Director, Creative Director who can define, champion, and deliver the creative vision for our DTC businesses on a shared ecommerce platform, bringing some of the world's most beloved IP to life in commerce.
...
- Our team starts with a deep understanding of the fan and the customer — using insights, behavioral data, and performance signals to shape storefront experiences designed to convert. This role defines how our properties come to life across homepage storytelling, merchandising strategy, launch moments, and fan engagement across multiple DTC businesses and IP portfolios.
...
- Great commerce starts with knowing the customer. In this role, our team sets the creative vision across multiple DTC businesses, establishes a distinct storefront identity for each property, and translates marketing direction into storytelling that converts. The frameworks that keep every launch and product story clear, purposeful, and on-brand — that's what this role owns. We value leaders who move fluidly between strategy and execution, guiding teams across merchandising, content, and design while making sure every decision maps back to platform and business goals.
...
- We test, learn, and improve — continuously. Our team connects creative direction to measurable outcomes and uses data and customer insights to refine the fan experience over time. We do our best work together, and this role collaborates closely with Marketing, Product, Design, Engineering, and senior leadership to bring fans closer to the stories they love!
...
- 12 or more years of creative leadership in DTC, ecommerce, digital media, entertainment, or consumer brands
- Ownership of end-to-end digital storefront creative strategy, including translating marketing direction into commerce-driven experiences
- Understanding of mobile-first and responsive digital environments
- Analytical mindset with the ability to translate data, customer insights, and performance outcomes into creative strategy
...
- Experience implementing test-and-learn operating models in ecommerce environments
- Executive presence with the ability to influence senior stakeholders
- Experience leading multidisciplinary teams
...
- Nice-to-haves
- Experience building storefront identities within franchise-driven or IP-led ecosystems on a shared commerce platform
- Comfort operating in lean, high-accountability team structures
- Familiarity with enterprise ecommerce platforms such as Salesforce Commerce Cloud
- Experience partnering closely with Marketing organizations at scale

results.title: Lee Tympanick
highlights:
Quality Analytics & Automation Engineer | Leveraging AI in Quality Workflows
...
Quality Analytics & Automation Engineer at [Hasbro](https://www.linkedin.com/company/hasbro)
...
I started out in product quality and compliance, spending several years at Hasbro making sure new games and toys were safe, reliable, and compliant across a wide range of brands. That work taught me how much rigor goes into getting a product right and how often the real bottleneck isn't the standards themselves, but the manual, fragmented systems used to manage them. That's what pulled me toward the technical side. I'm a fast, self-directed learner, and I taught myself to build real solutions with Microsoft Power Platform and AI — turning slow, spreadsheet-bound quality processes into automated, data-driven systems. As a Quality Analytics & Automation Engineer, I now design tools for risk assessment, product information management and tracking key project deliverables. More recently, I have been exploring ways to embed modern AI models directly into compliance workflows to assist in assessment. What excites me most is the intersection of deep domain knowledge and a tech-forward mindset — picking up new tools quickly, working out where they genuinely add value, and using them to make quality work faster, smarter, and more reliable. I'm always chasing the next skill to learn and the next process worth reinventing.
...

I build Power Platform and AI solutions that make quality and compliance processes faster, smarter, and more reliable — embedding AI directly into business workflows.

In addition to existing responsibilities, was asked to undertake a review of the implementation process at Hasbro with a Global Consulting Company, reporting to CIO, IT leadership Team and IT Steering Committee. The review was to put together a recommendation to accelerate the current implementation process, to integrate a 3rd Party Global Systems Integrator using an outsourced model where appropriate. This also required the assessment of using managed services and how they could be implemented at Hasbro. Ultimately I was made responsible for the delivery of the implementations under the new approach, including managing the relationship with the 3rd Party Global Systems Integrator. The project involved managing up to 190 people in the delivery, including internal resources, business partners, and consultants from multiple consulting companies. The consulting firms included managing onshore and offshore consultants. Show less

results.title: Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist | Wfh
...
# Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist
...
Posted Jun 30, 2026
...
The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.
...
- Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Operations, along with product management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques.
...
- Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases.
...
- Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work.
...
- Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA.
...
- Work with production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance.
...
- Participate in other data science teams collaborating with your peers to support their projects.
...
- Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team.
...
- Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform.
...
- Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption.
...
- Qualifications
- PhD or Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.
...
- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
...
- 9+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
...
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
...
- Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus.
...
- Passion for collaboration, learn it all mindset and driving value with AI.

# Experienced Data Scientist – AI/Machine Learning Developer – Generative AI Platform Development
...
Join arenaflex, and you'll be part of a team that's changing the world one innovation at a time. We are currently seeking a talented and passionate Data Scientist to join our Cloud Technologies Application Platform team and help us build the future of developer experience through cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions.
...
Are you ready to take your career to the next level with a company that values innovation and excellence? arenaflex is looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our dynamic team as an AI/Machine Learning Developer. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and executing our artificial intelligence strategy to elevate our developer experience platform to unprecedented heights.
...
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of generative AI technology, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to develop innovative solutions that will transform how developers create applications at arenaflex. You'll leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and large language models to solve complex business challenges and drive measurable impact across the organization.
...
As a Data Scientist at arenaflex, you will be responsible for driving our AI initiatives and contributing to the development of next-generation developer tools. Your key responsibilities will include:
...
Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.
...
Build, train, and fine-tune machine learning models, including foundation models and large language models, to address various business problems and improve developer productivity.
...
Choose appropriate machine learning algorithms, train models, and calibrate hyperparameters to achieve optimal predictive performance and model accuracy.
...
Apply deep expertise in transformer architecture, vector embeddings, and advanced ML techniques to develop solutions that solve complex business challenges.
...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Experience with training models or utilizing foundation models and fine-tuning them to meet custom business requirements.
...
Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related
...
Minimum of 5+ years of progressive experience in the software industry with a focused specialization in machine learning and artificial intelligence.
...
Strong understanding and extensive experience with cutting-edge machine learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, statistical modeling, and large language models.
...
- Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field

- Experience applying machine learning techniques specifically in developer productivity or software development lifecycle contexts

- Knowledge of cloud platforms and infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

- Familiarity with MLOps practices and deployment pipelines

- Published research or contributions to open-source machine learning projects

- Experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization techniques
...
If you are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and want to work on projects that will shape the future of technology, arenaflex is the place for you. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.

Senior ML engineer on Walmart's Catalog Data Science team building production GenAI and deep learning models for product attribute extraction, classification, and catalog optimization at petabyte scale.

About Team The Catalog Data Science Team at Walmart Global Tech is focused on using the latest research in generative AI (GenAI), artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), statistics, deep learning, computer vision and optimization to implement solutions that ensure Walmart’s product catalog is accurate, complete, and optimized for customer experience. Our team tackles complex data science and ML engineering challenges related to product classification, attribute extraction, trust & safety, and catalog optimization, empowering next-generation retail use cases. We take pride in writing solid production code in Python, deploying and supporting model services and pipelines, and pushing the boundaries in latency, throughput, and scalability.
...
As a Senior Data Scientist (Machine Learning Engineer) on the Catalog Data Quality team, you will collaborate with other Data Scientists and ML Engineers to develop, deploy, and scale machine learning models in production to extract attributes from product information. You will play a key role in supporting the attribute extraction platform and catalog data quality solutions — adding capabilities and driving adoption of GenAI-powered solutions to increase the coverage and accuracy of the Walmart's product catalog. The Data Science and ML Engineering community at Walmart Global Tech is active in most of the Hack events, utilizing the petabytes of data at our disposal, to build some of the coolest ideas. All the work we do at Walmart Global Tech will eventually benefit our operations & our associates, helping Customers Save Money to Live Better.

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
...
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
...
- Build and deploy production ML systems end-to-end: data pipelines, feature stores, model training, serving layers, monitoring, and feedback loops at scale
...
- Provide technical visionand lead advanced development in agentic AI, reinforcement learning, and simulation.
...
- Architect and deploy large-scale AI systems and autonomous AI agents — conversational assistants, predictive agents with tool-calling, and multi-agent orchestration systems using LangChain, Pydantic AI, and RAG patterns
...
- Deploy NLP pipelines at enterprise scale — semantic similarity across millions of records using BERT/SBERT embeddings and vector search (FAISS)
...
- Deep expertise in ML algorithms (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, AutoML) and deep learning (PyTorch, transformer architectures).
...
- Production experience with NLP (BERT, SBERT, FAISS, RAG), computer vision (YOLO, CLIP), and time series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet).
...
- Hands-on experience building and orchestrating multiple AI agents with LangChain, RAG, tool integration, and memory management.
...
- Multi-cloud ML platform proficiency — AWS SageMaker AND GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, BigQuery ML).
...
- Strong Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) with containerization (Docker) and MLOps practices.
...
- Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field AND 8+ years’ experience in data science or Machine Learning roles with demonstrated impact.
...
- PhD in AI, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, or related field with relevant industry experience.

- Experience with machine learning or deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face, MONAI, or similar tools.

- LLMs, vision-language models, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation, clinical NLP, or structured information extraction.
- Medical image analysis, including segmentation, object detection, registration, image classification, weak supervision, or foundation models.

The candidate will join a collaborative research environment at Boston Children’s Hospital and Harvard Medical School, working at the interface of artificial intelligence, medical imaging, clinical LLMs, multimodal learning, and pediatric medicine. Interested candidates should send a CV, a brief statement of research interests, and examples of relevant code, publications, or projects to: sila.kurugol@childrens.harvard.edu
